jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer likely crashing or being injured
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated to his character but lacks originality due to overuse

jokes.text: I hate to be a killjoy, but do you really think we can win playing 'Stars and Stripes Forever'? It's so Beginner Band. And we're Advanced Beginner Band.
jokes.setup: Lisa critiquing the band's song choice as too basic
jokes.punchline: The absurd distinction between 'Beginner Band' and 'Advanced Beginner Band'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of arbitrary school classifications with Lisa's characteristic overachieving perfectionism

jokes.text: You mean something... just arranged by Sousa? - No, something fresh and bold. - Well, darling, you have lost me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about playing non-Sousa music
jokes.punchline: Teacher's complete inability to comprehend music not connected to Sousa
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing teacher's rigid musical worldview

jokes.text: Hey, Grandpa, the Civil War's over.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting 'Camptown Races'
jokes.punchline: Bart pointing out the song's historical irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective generational humor with Bart's typical irreverence

jokes.text: Hey, the blonde broad's got a point. We need something with a little ring-a-ding-ding. - Take your arm off me. - Ooh, this kitten's got claws.
jokes.setup: Creepy older man hitting on Lisa inappropriately
jokes.punchline: His sleazy response to her rejection
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable inappropriate behavior played for laughs, dated and problematic

jokes.text: I.D.? Man, Charlie didn't ask for I.D. when I fought at La Choy and Chun King. I saw my best friend's head explode at Margaret Cho.
jokes.setup: Veteran claiming military service for discount
jokes.punchline: Confusing Asian food brands and comedian Margaret Cho with Vietnam War battles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation mixing Asian stereotypes with fake war trauma, perfectly structured buildup

jokes.text: Homer, give him the 50 cents. - Why should I? Did my country give me a parade? - No, man. They spat at me and- Just go. - Thank you. This closes the saddest chapter in American history.
jokes.setup: Homer reluctantly paying the fake veteran
jokes.punchline: Veteran's grandiose declaration about 50 cents closing America's saddest chapter
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the fake veteran bit with Homer's characteristic cheapness

jokes.text: Folks, how often have you opened your morning paper only to have the rubber band fly off and hit you right in the eye? - Never. But it's my number-one concern.
jokes.setup: Salesman describing a non-existent problem
jokes.punchline: Homer admitting it never happens but is still his top worry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - admitting something never happened while being worried about it anyway

jokes.text: A little late for Lenny.
jokes.setup: Lenny getting hit in the eye during safety goggle demonstration
jokes.punchline: Homer's callous observation about the ironic timing
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Homer's characteristic insensitivity

jokes.text: It's B.T.O. They're Canada's answer to E.L.P. Their big hit was 'T.C.B.' That's how we talked in the '70s. We didn't have a moment to spare.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining the band B.T.O.
jokes.punchline: Claiming '70s people abbreviated everything because they had no time
jokes.explanation: Clever absurd logic about '70s abbreviations with good period detail

jokes.text: No talkin'! No new crap! 'Takin' Care of Business' now!
jokes.setup: Band trying to play new material
jokes.punchline: Aggressive demand for only the hit song
jokes.explanation: Good satire of crowds wanting only familiar hits

jokes.text: I stand by my disappointed groan.
jokes.setup: Homer groaning about Lisa's band performance, Marge correcting him
jokes.punchline: Homer defending his negative reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stubbornness and meta-awareness of his own reaction

jokes.text: Check out Lisa. She's a sax machine. Makes me think I should have done something with my life. Oh, well. [Slurps]
jokes.setup: Homer praising Lisa's musical talent
jokes.punchline: Brief moment of self-reflection immediately dismissed
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer emotional arc - brief insight immediately abandoned

jokes.text: Makes me think I should have done something with my life. Oh. well. [Slurps]
jokes.setup: Watches Lisa play saxophone beautifully
jokes.punchline: Brief moment of self-reflection immediately dismissed with drink
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfect Barney character moment - fleeting awareness followed by immediate avoidance

jokes.text: Beautiful. Just beautiful. This is what 'comptrolling' is all about.
jokes.setup: Judge praising the musical performance
jokes.punchline: Using the made-up word 'comptrolling' seriously
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay humor, not particularly strong

jokes.text: Anybody got a building permit? 'Cause we nailed it.
jokes.setup: Celebrating good performance
jokes.punchline: Construction pun about 'nailing it'
jokes.explanation: Weak forced pun, very predictable construction metaphor

jokes.text: That is the best version of 'Living in America' I've ever heard. - Third best for me.
jokes.setup: First judge giving high praise
jokes.punchline: Second judge's oddly specific lower ranking
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between judges' reactions, realistic character difference

jokes.text: 'Stars and Stripes Forever.' Oh, man, they're toast.
jokes.setup: Ogdenville playing the basic song Lisa criticized earlier
jokes.punchline: Lisa's confident dismissal that will be proven wrong
jokes.explanation: Sets up the ironic reversal when Ogdenville wins with glow sticks

jokes.text: Glow sticks? But visual aids are against the rules.
jokes.setup: Ogdenville using glow sticks during performance
jokes.punchline: Lisa's rules-focused objection to obvious cheating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's rule-following nature

jokes.text: Well, it's not going to be easy to pick a winner. But I think one school red, white and blew us all away!
jokes.setup: Judge deliberating on winner
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'blue' vs 'blew' with patriotic colors
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pun but nothing special

jokes.text: I hope you enjoy your plaque, cheater!
jokes.setup: Lisa angry about losing to rule-breakers
jokes.punchline: Her bitter, unsportsmanlike outburst
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's competitive side overriding her usual politeness

jokes.text: Well, I was going to give you the Good Sportsmanship Award but now I'm just going to be sick.
jokes.setup: Judge responding to Lisa's outburst
jokes.punchline: Withdrawing award and claiming nausea
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Lisa's poor sportsmanship consequences

jokes.text: Look, Lisa! It glows! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about judges being impressed by glow sticks
jokes.punchline: Homer being equally impressed by the simple visual effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - being impressed by exactly what Lisa is criticizing

jokes.text: You mean state unfair. - Yeah, right. That's what I meant, Lisa. State unfair. - Zing!
jokes.setup: Lisa making a bitter pun about the 'state fair'
jokes.punchline: Marge missing the joke, Bart celebrating it
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic with each character reacting appropriately to Lisa's pun

jokes.text: See, Marge? I told you they could deep-fry my shirt. - I didn't say they couldn't. I said you shouldn't.
jokes.setup: Homer proving something could be deep-fried
jokes.punchline: Marge's distinction between 'could' and 'should'
jokes.explanation: Great married couple dynamic - Homer missing the point entirely

jokes.text: See, Marge? I told you they could deep-fry my shirt.
jokes.setup: Context of state fair food
jokes.punchline: Homer proving anything can be deep-fried
jokes.explanation: Absurd but believable Homer experiment with fair food

jokes.text: I wish people would stop telling me to 'step right up.'
jokes.setup: Carnival barker saying 'step right up'
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific complaint about the phrase
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about carnival cliches

jokes.text: You gotta stop being so trusting, Chief. - I'd rather let a thousand guilty men go free than chase after them.
jokes.setup: Lou criticizing Wiggum's gullibility
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's lazy misunderstanding of justice principles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Wiggum logic - twisting noble legal principle into excuse for laziness

jokes.text: Good luck getting a horse to eat dog food.
jokes.setup: Chief saying horse will go to dog food factory
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal misunderstanding of the process
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literal thinking - missing the obvious meaning

jokes.text: Excuse me, but I believe this family already had a horse and the expense forced Homer to work at the Kwik-E-Mart with hilarious consequences. - Anybody care what this guy thinks? - [All] No!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy pointing out show continuity
jokes.punchline: Everyone rejecting his pedantic observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about show continuity and fan complaints

jokes.text: Well, except the mop. [Slurping, Crunching]
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer doesn't have to feed fair purchases
jokes.punchline: Revealing someone is eating the mop
jokes.explanation: Good absurd visual gag with perfect timing

jokes.text: Marge, your pro-mop/anti-horse agenda has been clear for some time.
jokes.setup: Discussion about horse expenses vs fair purchases
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid conspiracy theory about Marge's preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer paranoia - creating elaborate conspiracy from simple disagreement

jokes.text: Now let's look in the rule book and see if horses can play in the NFL. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer praising Duncan's football-like performance
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing the obvious impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment with classic D'oh! realization

jokes.text: Dear Mr. Rresident - What are you doing, Lisa? - I'm writing a letter to President Clinton about that travesty of a band contest.
jokes.setup: Lisa writing formal letter
jokes.punchline: Her mispronunciation of 'President' while being so serious
jokes.explanation: Child-like error undermining Lisa's serious political action

jokes.text: It's the only fire extinguisher endorsed by both Lynda Carter and George Foreman. - I wonder what would happen if they had a baby.
jokes.setup: Marge listing celebrity endorsements
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre tangent about their hypothetical offspring
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non-sequitur from mundane product endorsement

jokes.text: Son, I learned everything I needed to know from The Horse Whisperer. Step one: Seduce a lonely housewife.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to know horse training from movie
jokes.punchline: Confusing the romantic subplot with actual horse training
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - focusing on wrong part of movie plot

jokes.text: [Whispering] When the race starts, run really fast.
jokes.setup: Homer doing 'horse whispering'
jokes.punchline: His advice is completely obvious and unhelpful
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to horse whispering setup with Homer's useless advice

jokes.text: You're... beau-ti-ful.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to pick up woman at track
jokes.punchline: His awkward, drawn-out delivery
jokes.explanation: Weak payoff to earlier Horse Whisperer joke, fairly predictable

jokes.text: Just once I'd like to ride clockwise. - Really? I thought I was the only one.
jokes.setup: Complaint about counterclockwise horse racing
jokes.punchline: Another jockey sharing this oddly specific desire
jokes.explanation: Good absurd complaint about arbitrary racing convention

jokes.text: Hey, where do you get those metal dealies for his feet? - You mean horseshoes? - Hey, what's with the attitude? I just want some dealies.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about horseshoes using wrong terminology
jokes.punchline: Getting defensive when corrected, insisting on 'dealies'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stubbornness - refusing proper terminology

jokes.text: That horse better win or we're taking a trip to the glue factory. And he won't get to come. - Yeah, that's a great tour. But you can't see it all in one day.
jokes.setup: Moe threatening horse with glue factory
jokes.punchline: Barney treating it like a tourist attraction recommendation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with Barney's oblivious tourist mindset

jokes.text: He hasn't even left the starting gate.
jokes.setup: Duncan expected to do poorly
jokes.punchline: He's doing even worse than expected - not moving at all
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Duncan's poor performance

jokes.text: What's this? Number five. Duncan... the horse no one expected to do anything. isn't doing anything.
jokes.setup: Duncan hasn't left starting gate
jokes.punchline: Announcer's deadpan observation about meeting low expectations
jokes.explanation: Sharp announcer wit about expectations being met in worst way possible

jokes.text: What a shame the race is already over.
jokes.setup: Duncan finally starts racing with great heart
jokes.punchline: His impressive effort is completely pointless
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing with Duncan's belated effort

jokes.text: Oh, wait. You were number five? You! You stink! - Damn good.
jokes.setup: Homer praising someone he thought was Duncan
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching to insults when realizing it was Duncan
jokes.explanation: Good Homer flip-flop based on mistaken identity

jokes.text: All those other horses will be shaking in their horse dealies.
jokes.setup: Homer describing Duncan's new tough attitude
jokes.punchline: Still calling horseshoes 'dealies'
jokes.explanation: Good callback maintaining Homer's stubborn terminology

jokes.text: Ladies, say hello to Furious D! He's the bad boy of racing. He's got attitude and 'baditude', so show him some latitude, and you'll win his gratitude. Only in America!
jokes.setup: Homer introducing the horse's new persona
jokes.punchline: Forced rhyme scheme ending with random patriotic exclamation
jokes.explanation: Overwrought wordplay typical of Homer's attempts at cleverness

jokes.text: Ew! You used my bracelet for a nose ring. - Possessions are fleeting.
jokes.setup: Lisa discovering her bracelet was repurposed
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-philosophical justification
jokes.explanation: Good Homer deflection with fake wisdom

jokes.text: That's my third monocle this week. I simply must stop being so horrified.
jokes.setup: Wealthy character shocked by Furious D's appearance
jokes.punchline: Recognizing his expensive shock reactions as a problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant aristocrat logic - treating horror as expensive habit

jokes.text: Can't I just bet that all the horses will have a fun time? - Yeah, I think you want that line.
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to make wholesome bet
jokes.punchline: Clerk directing her elsewhere for her naive request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge naivety about gambling

jokes.text: Mom, I think you might be developing a gambling problem. - Hey, I'm watching you.
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out Marge's complex betting
jokes.punchline: Marge's defensive paranoid response
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal with Marge becoming defensive

jokes.text: Mom, I think you might be developing a gambling problem. Hey, I'm watching you.
jokes.setup: Marge places complex racing bet
jokes.punchline: Lisa's concern vs Marge's defensive reaction
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal with Marge becoming the problematic one

jokes.text: Ah, man! That horse don't take no guff from nobody. - Guff? - I mean sh-
jokes.setup: Person praising horse's toughness using mild language
jokes.punchline: Self-correcting to stronger language then cutting off
jokes.explanation: Mild language escalation joke, fairly predictable

jokes.text: Man, I got more trophies than Wayne Gretzky and the pope combined.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating Duncan's wins
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison including the pope's trophy count
jokes.explanation: Great Homer absurdism - including pope in sports comparison

jokes.text: Why are you talking like that? - [High-pitched Voice] These are our real voices. [Normal Voice] We only talk like this on your surface world.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning jockeys' high voices
jokes.punchline: Revelation they fake normal voices for 'surface world'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fantasy concept - jockeys as underground creatures

jokes.text: All a lie. What woman would marry us? We're freaks. - [All] Freaks. Freaks. Freaks.
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning jockeys marry beauty queens
jokes.punchline: Jockeys' self-loathing chant
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about self-perception and societal expectations

jokes.text: All a lie. What woman would marry us? We're freaks. [All] Freaks. Freaks. Freaks.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions jockeys marry beauty queens
jokes.punchline: Bitter truth about their self-perception with chanting
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective subversion of jockey success mythology

jokes.text: We'll eat your brain. - My horse must lose.
jokes.setup: Jockeys threatening Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate terrified compliance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer's cowardly instant capitulation

jokes.text: Did that really happen, or was it just a wonderful dream? - No dream. Lose the race, fat boy.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning if jockey encounter was real
jokes.punchline: Jockey confirming it's real with insult
jokes.explanation: Standard dream/reality confusion with added insult

jokes.text: Well, it is a good life. Believe me. [Laughs]
jokes.setup: Bart saying Duncan could be a stud if he wins
jokes.punchline: Homer's knowing endorsement of stud life
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo typical of Homer's character

jokes.text: Welcome to the Springfield Derby, the fifth and penultimate duel of racing's Triple Crown.
jokes.setup: Formal race introduction
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'penultimate' when Triple Crown only has three races
jokes.explanation: Subtle math joke about misusing 'penultimate'

jokes.text: Could it be? In a bizarre twist, a horse is abusing a jockey. Might this be the start of a terrifying planet of the horses?
jokes.setup: Duncan attacks other jockeys during race
jokes.punchline: Announcer's dramatic speculation about role reversal apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Creative Planet of the Apes reference with good escalation from single incident to apocalyptic speculation

jokes.text: Might this be the start of a terrifying planet of the horses? In this announcer's opinion, almost certainly yes. And away I go.
jokes.setup: Horse abusing jockey during race
jokes.punchline: Announcer's apocalyptic interpretation and panicked exit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from simple role reversal to science fiction panic

jokes.text: I can't stress enough how easy it was to win this prestigious race. And furthermore- Hollywood is leading our kids down a moral sewer. Gotta go!
jokes.setup: Bart giving victory interview
jokes.punchline: Suddenly launching into moral panic then fleeing
jokes.explanation: Great non-sequitur from sports interview to moral outrage

jokes.text: I can't stress enough how easy it was to win this prestigious race. And furthermore- Hollywood is leading our kids down a moral sewer. Gotta go!
jokes.setup: Bart giving victory interview
jokes.punchline: Abrupt shift to moral commentary followed by hasty exit
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from boasting to random moral panic, fits Bart's age-inappropriate concerns

jokes.text: We'll give you gold. - Son, go get me a trash bag.
jokes.setup: Captured jockey offering treasure
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical disposal request
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Homer's casual approach to disposal

jokes.text: We'll give you gold. Now that you're a champion. you have your pick of the fillies. [Shudders] Yeah. she's the best.
jokes.setup: Jockeys offer bribes, mention horse breeding
jokes.punchline: Homer's disturbed reaction to whatever filly he's shown
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag implied by Homer's reaction, competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: Ooh, can you imagine- her face on her body?
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on horse breeding options
jokes.punchline: His disturbing physical combination fantasy
jokes.explanation: Mildly disturbing Homer comment, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Yeah, hi. I'm here to see Lisa Simpson. - [Gasps] You read my letter?
jokes.setup: Doorbell rings unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: It's actually the President responding to Lisa's letter
jokes.explanation: Great absurd escalation - President actually responding personally

jokes.text: If things don't go your way, just keep complaining until your dreams come true. - That's a pretty lousy lesson. - Hey, I'm a pretty lousy president.
jokes.setup: Clinton giving life advice to kids
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's bad advice because he's a bad president
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire with self-aware presidential incompetence

jokes.text: That's a pretty lousy lesson. Hey. I'm a pretty lousy president.
jokes.setup: Lisa objects to Clinton's lesson
jokes.punchline: Clinton's self-aware admission of presidential inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Good political humor with Clinton's characteristic self-deprecation as defense mechanism

jokes.text: Worst episode ever.
jokes.setup: End of episode
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's signature dismissive critique
jokes.explanation: Classic Comic Book Guy catchphrase, perfect character integration but overused
